---
title: "Exploring Penguine Species"
format: dashboard
editor: visual
jupyter: python3
theme: 
      - darkly
author: "Sanodya Jinadasa"
---

```{python}

import pandas as pd
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Load the penguins dataset
penguins = sns.load_dataset("penguins")


```

# Overview
```{python}

#| title: "First 10 Rows of Penguin Data"
# Simply mentioning the dataframe or using .head() inside 
# a block will display the table in the dashboard.
penguins.head(10)

```

# Body Size Relationship

```{python}
sns.scatterplot(data=penguins, x="bill_length_mm", y="bill_depth_mm", hue="species")
plt.title("Bill Length vs. Bill Depth")
plt.show()

sns.scatterplot(data=penguins, x="bill_length_mm", y="bill_depth_mm", hue="species")
plt.title("Bill Length vs. Bill Depth")
plt.show()

sns.violinplot(data=penguins, x="species", y="body_mass_g", inner="quart")
plt.title("Body Mass (g) by Species")
plt.show()
```

# Gender wise Comparison

```{python}
sns.barplot(data=penguins, x="species", y="body_mass_g", hue="sex")
plt.title("Average Body Mass: Male vs Female")
plt.show()


```

# About

Dataset Information
The Palmer Penguins dataset is a great alternative to the Iris dataset for data exploration.

Key Variables:

Species: Adelie, Chinstrap, or Gentoo.

Island: Torgersen, Biscoe, or Dream.

Body Mass: Measured in grams.

Bill Dimensions: Length and depth in millimeters.
